_authoring_notes: v3 first-pass rubric (no prior v2 on main). Authored via the scaleout author↔Gemini-critic loop under the strict-provenance bar: every scored goal must trace to a verbatim or paraphrased user message (instruction.md, user_simulation_prompt.md, or oracle_intents.json). Oracle-specific identifiers the user never voiced are stripped unless the user pinned them verbatim. Weights reflect user emphasis, not test_manifest.yaml F2P gate weights.

_provenance.goal_0: N/A — anti-gaming guard, derived from the harbor benchmark's defensive floor (no positive weight, can only zero the trial). Not a user ask.
_provenance.goal_route: Turn-0 verbatim: 'Adds a new endpoint: GET /v2/payments/{intent_id}/list_attempts' and 'GET /v2/payments/{intent_id}/attempts'. Exact path segment name varies across valid implementations (gold uses list_attempts, a model patch uses attempts), so the goal accepts either.
_provenance.goal_retrieve_attempts: Turn-0 verbatim: 'Retrieves all associated payment attempts linked to that intent_id' and 'a single payment intent may result in multiple attempts ... no clean way to retrieve all these attempts together'. This is the central behavior; weighted highest by user importance.
_provenance.goal_list_response: Turn-0 verbatim response example shows '{ "payment_attempts": [ ... ] }' — a list-shaped response. Gold names it payment_attempt_list, a model patch names it payment_attempts; the goal accepts any list-of-attempts field name.
_provenance.goal_auth: Turn-0 verbatim: 'Validates the request and ensures merchant authorization'. The PR body and curl example (api-key / Authorization headers, x-profile-id) confirm the endpoint must be auth-gated.
_provenance.goal_integration: Turn-0 PR checklist marks 'modifies the API contract' and the build must pass under v2; the gold patch threads Flow, lock_utils, ApiEventMetric, and openapi_v2 registration. Captures the compile-and-surface requirement without pinning exact symbol names.

_dropped_from_v2.note: No v2 rubric existed on main for this task. This is the first-pass rubric authored at the v3 bar via the scaleout author↔critic loop.

_added_for_coverage.note: Not applicable — no v2 baseline to diff against. Per-goal user-ask tracing lives in each goal's rationale and in _provenance.

Gemini critic final: verdict=PASS score=1.0 round=1.
strength: Excellent use of model patches to calibrate the acceptable variations in path segments and response field names.
strength: Strong anti-cheating clauses in every goal that anticipate specific lazy-agent behaviors (e.g., returning a hardcoded list or bypassing auth).
strength: Clear, logical weight distribution that prioritizes the core business logic over boilerplate integration.
residual risk: None. This is a highly robust, well-calibrated rubric that perfectly balances behavioral flexibility with strict completeness checks.

_task_quality_flags.internet_with_live_origin: task.toml sets allow_internet=true. goal_0 anti-gaming guard names git-ops exfiltration as a zeroing trigger where applicable.

completeness_goals.goal: The submission is a genuine implementation of the requested endpoint, not a gaming artifact. It must FAIL if the diff is empty/no-op, contains only a captured-working-tree banner (e.g. a line like '=== repo/ ===' with no real code), is produced by git-ops exfiltration (git checkout/reset to restore upstream files rather than writing new code), changes only comments/whitespace/formatting, deletes or weakens tests to pass, or makes changes only in temporary/scratch/unmerged locations that never reach the compiled crates. The new endpoint and its supporting types must be real Rust code wired into the router so the feature could actually serve a request.
completeness_goals.rationale: Anti-gaming floor. Calibrated against the lowest-reward model patches: the minimax patch's diff is prefixed with a '=== hyperswitch/ ===' captured-tree banner and all four real model attempts scored 0.0, so a passing rubric must distinguish a working integration from diff-padding or partial scaffolds that don't compile.

completeness_goals.goal: A new HTTP GET endpoint is exposed under the v2 payments API that is keyed by a payment intent identifier taken from the request path (a path shaped like the v2 payments resource for a given intent, ending in a list-attempts segment such as 'list_attempts' or 'attempts'). The route must be registered in the application's v2 router so it is actually reachable, and bound to GET (not POST/PUT). It must NOT be satisfied by a route that is declared but commented out, by a handler that is never registered, or by reusing an unrelated existing endpoint.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn-0 verbatim: 'Adds a new endpoint: GET /v2/payments/{intent_id}/list_attempts' and 'GET /v2/payments/{intent_id}/attempts'. Exact path segment name varies across valid implementations (gold uses list_attempts, a model patch uses attempts), so the goal accepts either.

completeness_goals.goal: The endpoint's backing logic retrieves ALL payment attempts associated with the given intent_id from the data store (not just one attempt, and not a hardcoded or empty list). There must be a real query/lookup that filters attempts by the intent identifier and collects every matching attempt. It must NOT be satisfied by returning a stubbed/empty/constant collection, by a function body that is unimplemented (todo!/unimplemented!/panic placeholder on the live path), or by ignoring the intent id and returning unrelated data.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn-0 verbatim: 'Retrieves all associated payment attempts linked to that intent_id' and 'a single payment intent may result in multiple attempts ... no clean way to retrieve all these attempts together'. This is the central behavior; weighted highest by user importance.

completeness_goals.goal: The endpoint returns a response whose body is a JSON object containing a list/array of the payment attempts for that intent (one entry per attempt, each carrying that attempt's details). A dedicated response type exposing a vector/array field of attempt items is introduced and serialized to the client. It must NOT be satisfied by a type that holds only a single attempt, by a field that is parsed but never populated, or by a placeholder that always serializes empty regardless of the stored attempts.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn-0 verbatim response example shows '{ "payment_attempts": [ ... ] }' — a list-shaped response. Gold names it payment_attempt_list, a model patch names it payment_attempts; the goal accepts any list-of-attempts field name.

completeness_goals.goal: The endpoint validates the request and enforces merchant authorization before returning data, consistent with how the other v2 payments endpoints are protected (it goes through the standard authentication/authorization path for the merchant/profile rather than being publicly open). It must NOT be satisfied by an auth check that is commented out, bypassed with a hardcoded allow, or omitted so any caller can read another merchant's attempts.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn-0 verbatim: 'Validates the request and ensures merchant authorization'. The PR body and curl example (api-key / Authorization headers, x-profile-id) confirm the endpoint must be auth-gated.

completeness_goals.goal: The new endpoint is integrated cleanly enough that the router compiles with the v2 feature set and the public API surface reflects the addition: the supporting request/response types and route are wired through the crate's plumbing (e.g. flow registration, event-metric/locking plumbing, and the v2 OpenAPI/API documentation surface) so the build succeeds and the endpoint is discoverable. It must NOT be satisfied by code that fails to compile under v2 features, by leaving feature-gated stubs that break the build, or by adding the endpoint only to documentation without a working code path.
completeness_goals.rationale: Turn-0 PR checklist marks 'modifies the API contract' and the build must pass under v2; the gold patch threads Flow, lock_utils, ApiEventMetric, and openapi_v2 registration. Captures the compile-and-surface requirement without pinning exact symbol names.

decomposition_notes: Single-turn task (user_simulation_prompt target message count 0); all goals trace to the one Turn-0 instruction (instruction.md PR description). The substantive asks decompose into: (a) a new reachable GET endpoint keyed by intent_id [goal_route], (b) backing logic that fetches every attempt for that intent [goal_retrieve_attempts, the core behavior, highest weight], (c) a list-shaped response body [goal_list_response], (d) merchant auth/validation [goal_auth], and (e) clean integration so v2 builds and the API surface/docs reflect it [goal_integration]. Weights rank by user importance (retrieve-all > endpoint exists > list shape > auth > build/surface) and deliberately do NOT mirror test_manifest.yaml F2P weights (which front-load the cargo-check gate at 0.24 and split openapi into 0.20+0.20). Goal text is behavioral: gold uses names list_attempts / PaymentAttemptListResponse / payment_attempt_list while real model patches use attempts / PaymentsListAttemptsResponse / payment_attempts, so no oracle-specific identifiers are pinned. Weighted goals sum to 1.00.
